seoTitle: Port Bonython vs Port Broughton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Bonython and Port Broughton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Bonython vs Port Broughton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Bonython and Port Broughton.
heading: Port Bonython vs Port Broughton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Bonython, South Australia and Port Broughton, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Bonython has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Port Broughton (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Bonython or Port Broughton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Bonython has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Port Broughton scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Bonython scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Port Broughton scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Bonython or Port Broughton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Bonython has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
